message:
fix(pages/encrypt): POSIX fsync-the-parent for sync_tree [92o31]

Mock-finder bead 92o31 surfaced that `src/pages/encrypt.rs::sync_tree`
(the encrypted-HTML-archive durability flush) did NOT fsync the parent
directory after the subtree walk completed:

  #[cfg(not(windows))]
  fn sync_tree(path: &Path) -> Result<()> {
      sync_tree_inner(path)?;
      Ok(())       // ← missing parent-directory fsync
  }

Its sibling `src/pages/bundle.rs:457-461` (same name, same cfg gate,
same purpose for plain pages bundles) correctly chains
`sync_parent_directory(path)`.

POSIX fsync on a file flushes file contents + inode metadata but does
NOT flush the dirent in the parent directory that points at the file.
Without the parent-directory fsync, a power-loss between encrypt's
return and the next sync_all on the parent leaves the encrypted
archive's contents on disk but unreachable by path: operators see
"success" exit code and a missing file on remount.

This commit mirrors the proven `bundle.rs` shape:

1. New `sync_parent_directory` helper in encrypt.rs (cfg-gated like
   the existing platform pair). Linux variant opens the parent,
   calls `sync_all()`, wraps both error sites with Context that
   names the path and the "after encrypted export" operation —
   so a sync failure surfaces a useful diagnostic instead of bare
   errno. Windows variant is a no-op (NTFS journals dirent updates
   synchronously with file create/rename, so POSIX two-step is not
   portable).

2. `sync_tree` now chains `sync_parent_directory(path)` after the
   subtree walk.

3. Regression test `sync_tree_includes_parent_directory_fsync` pins
   both the happy path AND a negative-side guard: the test invokes
   `sync_tree` on a path whose parent directory was removed between
   file-creation and the fsync call, and asserts `sync_tree` SURFACES
   the I/O error rather than silently returning Ok(()). Before this
   fix, `sync_tree` would have silently succeeded (no parent-fsync
   to trigger the error), so the negative test proves the new stack
   frame is actually running — not just that the fix is cosmetic.

Verified: cargo test --lib pages::encrypt:: passes 12/12 including
the new regression test.

Refs coding_agent_session_search-92o31

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
